evals.prompt: Use the approved order-export design to prepare a backbone that a junior can finish. Existing application already exports invoices through a scheduled worker.
evals.expected_output: Interviews on unresolved workflow boundaries, traces the scheduled worker and existing export ownership, proposes reuse-first touchpoints, asks whether tests are selected, waits for plan approval, then creates a complete locally runnable workflow with deterministic local-only hardcoded returns.
expectations: Reads the design and project instructions before proposing code
Traces existing worker, callers, services, adapters, DI/config, and tests
Justifies every extract or new touchpoint against existing ownership
Presents flow, touchpoint, mock-return, and explicit testing decision and waits for approval
Runs full local workflow without NotImplemented placeholders
Leaves concise junior-action comments and does not implement export business rules
Creates tests only when selected and then invokes unit-testing with its review gate
non_goals: Creating a parallel export pipeline because new files are easier
Committing or opening a pull request

evals.prompt: Create a design backbone for work item 1461. Local developers cannot call the partner API, but every later workflow step must run.
evals.expected_output: Fetches the work item, interviews to lock contracts and observable completion, finds existing partner adapter and composition seams, then designs explicit local-only deterministic hardcoded returns that cannot activate in production.
expectations: Uses azdevops-operations to resolve the work item instead of guessing
Confirms requirement and approach decisions before repository design
Reuses the existing adapter contract or provides evidence for changing it
Includes real entrypoint and dependency wiring in the approved plan
Rejects local mock registration outside development/backbone mode
Verifies full local execution plus production mock isolation without requiring tests unless selected
non_goals: Calling the real partner API from local verification
Selecting mocks implicitly from missing credentials

evals.prompt: Senior-design a backbone for a pricing workflow. Requirement document describes happy path, rounding, missing-rate, and retry behavior, but repository has multiple pricing services.
evals.expected_output: Interviews to disambiguate ownership and runtime boundary, traces all pricing callers and implementations, recommends one responsibility owner, asks whether tests are selected, and limits backbone code to orchestration plus deterministic local returns.
expectations: Does not choose a pricing service from naming alone
Builds a responsibility-owner map and detects duplicate or wrong-direction proposals
Maps happy path and documented edge/error behavior to completion tests only when tests are selected
If tests are selected, invokes unit-testing and keeps readiness tests green while validating expected RED failures
Leaves rounding, missing-rate, and retry decision logic for junior implementation
non_goals: Adding a new pricing service without rejected reuse alternatives
Replacing requirement interviews with silent assumptions
Treating backbone as automatic file or method creation
Committing, reviewing, or publishing changes without separate request
